source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/00_기타 작업 서류/240718 - DK 제품별 자료 정리/DK_제품별 자료 정리(pdf)/4. A1417MCW/6. IEC Test Report (EMC, Safety)/PRJ0042257 REP028549 CBTR IEC60601-1 H&abyz A1717MCW (Original)_CSV.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: IEC 60601-1 safety test report section for a component power supply, covering marking, electrical protection, insulation, leakage current, earthing, mechanical hazards, radiation, temperature, and fire prevention clauses.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory safety evidence for IEC 60601-1 compliance and identifies items requiring additional evaluation in the end product application.
keyPoints: The equipment is repeatedly characterized as a component-level power supply requiring additional end-product evaluation for several accompanying-document, accessibility, mechanical, and fire-hazard requirements.

Electrical protection requirements in Clause 8 show pass verdicts for accessible-part limits, MOOP, protective earthing, leakage-current limits, insulation, creepage/clearance, mains isolation, fuses, and internal mains wiring.

No MOPP, applied parts, defibrillation-proof applied parts, external DC source connection, oxygen-rich-environment use, X-radiation, pressure vessels, or patient support systems are identified in this chunk.

entities.name: Report No. 269183
entities.evidence: Pages 27-70 headers state: "Page 27 of 138 Report No. 269183" and "IEC 60601-1".

entities.name: IEC 60601-1
entities.evidence: Each page header identifies the report section as "IEC 60601-1" with clause requirements and verdicts.

entities.name: Component power supply
entities.evidence: Multiple remarks state: "The equipment is a power supply unit, shall be additionally evaluated in the end product."

entities.name: IEC 60320 appliance inlet
entities.evidence: Clause 8.6.2 states the equipment is provided with an IEC 60320 appliance inlet and that the earth pin is considered the protective earth terminal.

entities.name: Y1 capacitors C1 and C2
entities.evidence: Clause 8.5.1.3 states: "Y1 capacitors (C1 and C2) are used across primary and protective earth."

entities.name: Power transformer T1
entities.evidence: Clause 8.8.2 states insulation tape of Power transformer T1 was tested according to appended Table 8.8.3.

entities.name: ISO 14971
entities.evidence: Several risk-management-file entries cite ISO 14971 clauses, including "ISO 14971 Cl. 4.2-4.4, 5, 6.2-6.5".

concepts.name: Means of Operator Protection (MOOP)
concepts.evidence: Clause 8.5.1.1 states operator protection is provided from mains to secondary; Clause 8.5.1.3 states "MOOP considered" with pass verdict.

concepts.name: No Means of Patient Protection (MOPP)
concepts.evidence: Clause 8.5.1.2 states "No MOPP" and related MOPP checks are N/A.

concepts.name: Protective earthing
concepts.evidence: Clause 8.6 covers protective and functional earthing; the appliance inlet earth pin is treated as the protective earth terminal.

concepts.name: Leakage current compliance
concepts.evidence: Clause 8.7 states leakage-current values are checked in appended Table 8.7 and touch current and earth leakage current requirements pass.

concepts.name: Creepage and air clearance compliance
concepts.evidence: Clause 8.9.1.1 states creepage distances and air clearances are equal to or greater than values in Tables 12 to 16, with pass verdict.

concepts.name: End product evaluation dependency
concepts.evidence: Many remarks state the component power supply shall be additionally evaluated in the end product application.

concepts.name: Fire enclosure construction
concepts.evidence: Clause 11.2.1 and 11.3 address enclosure strength and fire enclosure construction; internal wires and PCB material receive pass verdicts.

evidence.claim: The mains switch on/off positions are marked using IEC 60601-1 Table D.1 symbols 12 and 13.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 7.4.1: "Symbols 12 and 13 of table D.1 are marked on the mains switch." Verdict P.

evidence.claim: The device has green/yellow internal protective earth wiring and complies with conductor color requirements for protective earth connections.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 7.7.1: "All internal earth wiring is green and yellow." Clause 7.7.2 also has pass verdict.

evidence.claim: Accompanying-document requirements were not evaluated for the component power supply and must be determined in the end product.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 7.9.1: "Accompanying document is not provided with component power supply. Accompanying document requirements have not been evaluated. To be determined in the end product."

evidence.claim: The equipment is intended only for connection to supply mains, not a separate power source or external DC power source.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.2.1 says "Only intended to be connected with the supply mains"; Clause 8.2.2 says "No connection to an external d.c. power source."

evidence.claim: Accessible-part touch-current limits are evaluated by reference to appended Table 8.7 and pass.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.4.2(b): "LEAKAGE CURRENTS from, to, or between ACCESSIBLE PARTS did not exceed limits for TOUCH CURRENT: See appended Table 8.7" with verdict P.

evidence.claim: Residual voltage at the plug one second after disconnection did not exceed 60 V.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.4.3: worst case voltage after disconnecting plug refers to appended Table 8.4.3 with verdict P; following line states "Not exceeded 60 V."

evidence.claim: The power supply provides Means of Operator Protection from mains to secondary, but no Means of Patient Protection.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.5.1.1: "Operator protection (MOOP) provided; from mains to secondary." Clause 8.5.1.2: "No MOPP."

evidence.claim: Y1 capacitors C1 and C2 are used across primary and protective earth and are treated as two MOOP.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.5.1.3: "Y1 capacitors (C1 and C2) are used across primary and protective earth. And see appended Table 8.10." Verdict P.

evidence.claim: The device has no Type F, Type B, or defibrillation-proof applied parts in this evaluated section.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 8.5.2.1 and 8.5.2.2 state "No Type F-type applied part" and "No Type B applied part"; Clause 8.5.5 states "No defibrillation-proof applied parts."

evidence.claim: The protective earth terminal is implemented through an IEC 60320 appliance inlet with an internal crimp connection.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.6.2: "The equipment is provided with an IEC 60320 appliance inlet. Internal protective earth connection to the appliance inlet is by crimp connector at appliance inlet."

evidence.claim: Protective earth connection continuity passes, with impedance not exceeding 0.1 ohm noted for the relevant condition.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.6.4(a): "See appended Table 8.6.4" with verdict P; Clause 8.6.4(b) remarks "Impedance of protective earth connection does not exceed 0.1 ohm."

evidence.claim: The potential equalization terminal is accessible, detachable without a tool, not used for protective earth, and marked with Table D.1 symbol 8.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.6.7 lists accessible, detachable without a tool, not used for protective earth, and "Terminal marked with symbol 8 of Table D.1" with pass verdicts.

evidence.claim: Leakage current limits for touch current and earth leakage current pass against IEC 60601-1 Clause 8.7.3.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.7.3(c): touch current did not exceed 100 μA normal and 500 μA single fault; Clause 8.7.3(d): earth leakage did not exceed 5 mA normal and 10 mA single fault; both refer to appended Table 8.7 with verdict P.

evidence.claim: Solid insulation and dielectric strength requirements pass, with Power transformer T1 insulation tape tested.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.8.3 states insulating materials with safety function withstood dielectric strength test voltages; Clause 8.8.2 identifies insulation tape of Power transformer T1 tested in appended Table 8.8.3.

evidence.claim: Creepage distances and air clearances comply with IEC 60601-1 Tables 12 through 16.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.9.1.1: "CREEPAGE DISTANCES and AIR CLEARANCES are equal to or greater than values in Tables 12 to 16 (inclusive): See insulation diagram and associate table." Verdict P.

evidence.claim: Conductors and connectors are secured by through-hole soldered connections, and internal wiring is protected from sharp edges and moving parts.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.10.2: "Connections are through-hole and soldered." Clause 8.10.5: wiring is adequately protected against sharp edges or secured away from moving parts.

evidence.claim: The appliance inlet is used as the mains disconnecting device and as the means of isolating circuits from supply mains.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.11.1(a): "The appliance inlet is used as mains disconnecting device." Clause 8.11.1(f) also states the appliance inlet is used as mains disconnect device.

evidence.claim: The power supply cord set is not evaluated in this report, but a certified appliance coupler is provided.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 8.11.3.1-8.11.3.3 state the power supply cord set is not evaluated; Clause 8.11.3.4 states "Certified appliance coupler provided."

evidence.claim: Mains fuses are provided with adequate breaking capacity and no fuse is placed in the protective earth conductor.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.11.5: "Mains fuses provided"; "Protective devices have adequate breaking capacity"; "No fuse in protective earth conductor." Verdicts P.

evidence.claim: Mechanical hazard clauses are largely not applicable because the equipment is a power supply unit and has no moving parts, guards, emergency stops, patient constraints, pressure vessels, or support systems.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 9.2-9.8 repeatedly state no moving parts, no emergency stopping devices, no patient constraints, no pressure vessel, and that the power supply unit requires end-product evaluation.

evidence.claim: The equipment does not produce X-radiation and is not intended for hand-transmitted vibration or oxygen-rich-environment use.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 10.1.1: "Equipment does not produce X-radiation." Clause 9.6.3 says not intended for such vibration; Clause 11.2.2.1 says not intended for oxygen rich environments.

evidence.claim: Excessive temperature requirements pass for equipment parts, with additional end-product evaluation noted.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 11.1.1: temperatures did not exceed table values, referring to appended Tables 11.1.1 and 11.1.3; remark states the power supply unit shall be evaluated in the end product application. Verdict P.

evidence.claim: Fire enclosure material requirements pass for internal wires and PCB material.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 11.3(a): "Internal wires suitably rated" and "PCB material is rated as minimum V-1 of flammability" with pass verdicts.

reviewFlags.reason: This is chunk 8 of 13; extracted facts are limited to pages 27-70 shown in the chunk.
Many requirements are marked N/A or deferred because the item is a component-level power supply requiring additional evaluation in the end product application.
Several pass verdicts refer to appended tables or insulation diagrams that are not included in this chunk, so numeric test values are not available here.
